title: Ottawa Public Library Board - June 02, 2026
meeting_location: Champlain Room, 110 Laurier Avenue West, and by electronic participation

agenda_items.title: Notices and meeting information for meeting participants and the public
agenda_items.description: Notices and meeting information are attached to the agenda and minutes, including: availability of simultaneous interpretation and accessibility accommodations; in camera meeting procedures; information items not subject to discussion; personal information disclaimer for correspondents and public speakers; notices regarding minutes; and remote participation details.
agenda_items.minutes:
This draft Minutes document contains a summary of the disposition of items and actions taken at the meeting. This document does not include all of the text that will be included in the final Minutes, such as the record of written and oral submissions. Recorded votes and dissents contained in this draft Minutes document are draft until the Minutes of the meeting are confirmed by the Board. The final draft Minutes will be published with the agenda for the next regular Board meeting and, once confirmed, will replace this document.
Notes:
The Chair advised that OPL Board Governance Standing Committee Chair, Jessica Bradley had an additional item under Board Members' Items.
MOTION OPL 20260602/1
MOTION REQUIRING SUSPENSIONS OF THE RULES OF PROCEDURE
Moved by Jessica Bradley:
That the Ottawa Public Library Board waive the Rules of Procedure to approve the addition of the Service Agreements between Ottawa Public Library Board and City Council for consideration at today's meeting pursuant to Section 21(3) of the Rules of Procedure By-law (By-law, November 8, 2022).
Results: Carried
(This motion was moved following the items 11.2) on today's agenda).

sub_agendas_items.title: OPL Board Delegation of Authority Policy – Motion (Jessica Bradley, Chair, Governance Standing Committee)

attachments.attachment_title: OPLB Delegation of Authority Policy - Motion (Jessica Bradley, Chair, Gov Std Ctee) .pdf

sub_agendas_items.minutes:
MOTION OPL 20260602/7
WHEREAS in the OPL Q1 2025 Financial Status Report, approved by the OPL Board on May 13, 2025 (OPLB-2025-0513-10.2), the Board authorized an increase to spending authority limits for senior staff; and,
WHEREAS that resulted in a revision of the OPLB Delegation of Authority Policy (OPLB Policy 002, “the DOA Policy,” (Document 1, included herein) specifically the CEO’s delegated spending authority (Item 4) was increased to <$750,000; and,
WHEREAS the Board’s authority should have been amended accordingly in the DOA Policy to align with this change;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT the OPL Board approve a technical amendment revising the OPLB Delegation of Authority Policy, specifically, updating Item four (4) to reflect that the Board authority is required for expenditures >$750,000.
Results: Carried

sub_agendas_items.title: Service Agreements between Ottawa Public Library Board and City Council
sub_agendas_items.minutes:
MOTION OPL 20260602/9
WHEREAS  the Public Libraries Act provides that a municipality may establish a public library board, and where such a board is established, it is constituted as a separate statutory governing body with responsibility for the management and control of the library, including the oversight and stewardship of its financial affairs, the provision of library services, and the exercise of powers necessary to carry out its mandate, while operating at arm’s length from Council and in partnership with the municipality; and,
WHEREAS the Ottawa Public Library (OPL) Board and the City of Ottawa (City) have, since amalgamation, established a collaborative relationship that supports the delivery of services to residents, with shared services focused on defined administrative and operational functions, contributing to efficiencies and effective stewardship of public resources; and,
WHEREAS past governance and budget audits requested by the OPL Board and conducted by the City’s Auditor General in 2008 identified the need to clarify the Board’s arm’s-length relationship with the City through formalized mechanisms, as well as to articulate roles and responsibilities in the budget process, and subsequent governance actions by the Board, including the establishment of a Governance and Audit Committee (now the Governance Standing Committee) and updates to Board governance policies, have reinforced the importance of clearly articulated accountability frameworks and decision-making processes between the OPL Board and City Council; and,
WHEREAS existing Board policies and service level agreements (SLAs), have supported aspects of this relationship, no current agreement comprehensively defines the governance framework, including the SLAs; and,
WHEREAS other public library systems across Ontario have established memoranda of understanding (MOU) or similar agreements with their municipal partners to provide clarity, transparency, and consistency in governance and shared service delivery; and,
WHEREAS the development of a MOU, supported by complementary SLAs, would provide a transparent framework for governance, while respecting the legislative authority of both the Board and Council; and, 
WHEREAS advancing this work aligns with OPL’s commitment to integrity, accountability, and effective stewardship of public resources, and supports transparent, consistent, and well-coordinated reporting and decision-making; and, 
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT the Ottawa Public Library Board direct staff to continue to work collaboratively with City of Ottawa staff to develop a comprehensive Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the OPL Board and City Council, outlining governance principles, roles and responsibilities, and the framework for shared services; and,
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT the MOU be supported, as appropriate, by service level agreements (SLAs) between OPL and relevant City departments to clarify service expectations, accountability, and operational processes; and,
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT the MOU includes provisions for regular review and renewal aligned with the terms of the Board and Council, to ensure it remains current and responsive to evolving needs; and,
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT staff report back to the Board on progress toward the development of the MOU before the end of the current Board term in December 2026; and, 
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT the OPL Board share this motion with City Council.
Results: Carried
